acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `MIGRATION.md` includes legacy deps hard-cutover section with concrete commands.
- [ ] Docs include safe rollback and backup-ref guidance.
- [ ] Fleet rollout recipe is documented (and script/just target if implemented).
- [ ] Caveats/tradeoffs are documented for operators.
description:
**Problem**
Operators need a deterministic rollout/backfill workflow for mixed-format repos; current migration docs cover go-import but not legacy deps hard-cutover on existing store refs.

**Files**: MIGRATION.md, docs/plans/2026-03-01-legacy-deps-hard-cutover-design.md (reference), scripts/ (if rollout helper added)
design:
**Design**
Document and validate rollout recipe:
- detect -> dry-run -> migrate -> verify loop
- rollback/backup guidance for store ref
- fleet/multi-repo backfill command recipe
- explicit hard-cutover caveats (older binary incompatibility, provenance/deletion limits)

If a helper script/just target is added, ensure it is safe-by-default and dry-run friendly.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-10 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover: MIGRATION.md now documents detect/dry-run/execute/rollback/compatibility, but fleet rollout recipe and some operator caveats still need finishing.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Audit on 2026-03-11 against /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs-legacy-deps-v1-cutover @ 03adab51: MIGRATION.md now includes the hard-cutover operator playbook (detect -> dry-run -> migrate -> verify), automatic backup-ref guidance plus manual rollback snippet, a dry-run-first fleet rollout recipe, and explicit caveats for old-binary incompatibility, provenance collapse, deleted-edge limits, and checkpoint self-heal behavior. No helper script or just target was added.

title: Migration slice 8: Migration docs and fleet rollout recipe

description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_PLAN.md v0.5 says receivers must verify `prev_sha256` matches the current head before buffering/appending, but also mandates buffering out-of-order events and issuing WANT. For seq > durable+1, `prev_sha256` references seq-1 which is unknown until the gap fills, so the rule is ambiguous.

**Design**
Clarify the intended rule:
- Option A: allow buffering even if prev_sha is not yet verifiable; validate when the prefix arrives.
- Option B: require prev_sha to match the durable head (no buffering of future events).
Update plan text to remove ambiguity and align the replication/model implementations.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Spec text explicitly states when prev_sha must be validated for buffered events.
- [ ] Model/implementation updated to match clarified rule.

**Files:**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md
- beads_stateright_models/examples/canonical_hash_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/repl_core_machine.rs
title: Clarify prev_sha validation vs gap buffering in v0.5 replication

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tests are re-homed to the crates that own the code under test
- [ ] `crates/beads-rs` tests are assembly/e2e-focused only
- [ ] `core/bd-qicx` is resolved as part of this work or explicitly linked/closed by the implementation
- [ ] `cargo check -p beads-rs --all-features` passes
- [ ] `cargo test` passes for the moved test coverage
description:
**Problem**
The final boundary cleanup will only be durable if tests live with the crates that actually own the behavior. Today there is still cross-crate test harness logic under `beads-rs`, and there is a known package-level feature-wiring bug (`core/bd-qicx`) where `cargo check -p beads-rs --all-features` relies on workspace feature unification.

**Where**
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/**`
- `crates/beads-rs/src/test_harness/mod.rs`
- daemon-owned tests under `crates/beads-daemon/tests/**`
- git-owned tests under `crates/beads-git/**`
- package feature wiring in `crates/beads-rs/Cargo.toml`
- `core/bd-qicx`

**Why It Matters**
If the tests stay mis-owned, the code will drift back toward the wrong boundaries. The package-level all-features gap is also a real verification hole and should be closed as part of this cleanup.

**Files to Study**
- `crates/beads-rs/src/test_harness/mod.rs`
- `crates/beads-rs/tests/integration/**`
- `crates/beads-daemon/tests/**`
- `crates/beads-git/**`
- `crates/beads-rs/Cargo.toml`
- `bd show core/bd-qicx`
design: Move path/config tests to `beads-bootstrap`, git state-loading tests to `beads-git`, and keep daemon-internal behavior tests in daemon-owned crates. Leave `beads-rs` tests focused on assembly/e2e behavior. Fix the package-level all-features mismatch so `beads-rs` no longer relies on workspace feature unification.
title: Re-home tests to owner crates and fix the package-level all-features gap
